documentation.description:
On input, an EOL is any of a CR, LF, or CR-LF pair
On output, an EOL is an LF (TBD: allow other EOLs)

subprograms.name: End_Of_Line
subprograms.code: function End_Of_Line (File : in out File_Handle) return Boolean

documentation.description: Returns True if the next thing in File is an EOL; False otherwise

subprograms.name: New_Line
subprograms.code: procedure New_Line (File : in out File_Handle; Spacing : in Positive := 1)

subprograms.name: Next
subprograms.code: function Next (File : in out File_Handle) return Character

documentation.description: Skips any EOLs in File and then reads a single Character from File into Item

subprograms.name: Next_Line
subprograms.code: function Next_Line (File : in out File_Handle) return String

documentation.description:
Reads Characters from File until an EOL is encountered
Skips the EOL

subprograms.name: Read
subprograms.code: procedure Read (File : in out File_Handle; Item : out String)

documentation.description: Calls File.Next for every Character in Item

subprograms.name: Read_Line
subprograms.code: procedure Read_Line (File : in out File_Handle; Item : out String; Last : out Natural)

documentation.description:
Reads Characters from File until Item is filled or an EOL is encountered
The index of the last position filled in Item is put in Last
If an EOL is encountered, skips the EOL
If End_Of_Line (File), Last will be 0

subprograms.name: Skip_Line
subprograms.code: procedure Skip_Line (File : in out File_Handle; Spacing : in Positive := 1)

subprograms.name: Write
subprograms.code: procedure Write (File : in out File_Handle; Item : in Character)

subprograms.name: Write
subprograms.code: procedure Write (File : in out File_Handle; Item : in String)

documentation.description: Calls File.Write for every Character in Item

subprograms.name: Write_Line
subprograms.code: procedure Write_Line (File : in out File_Handle; Item : in String)

documentation.description: Writes Item to File followed by an EOL
